title: Architectures and systems for managing personal data to be utilized by dynamic sets of external parties
abstract: Techniques and architectures to manage personal data. Permissions are maintained information for one or more portions of the electronic personal record. Connection information for the one or more portions of the electronic personal record are maintained. At least one of the one or more portions of the electronic personal record information from a static document provided by the user and dynamic information obtained via an integration with an external data source. The one or more processors further to evaluate claims on portions of the electronic record from providers utilizing attribute-based security mechanisms. The corresponding portions of the electronic personal record are selectively provided in response to results of the evaluation.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A system comprising: a secure data storage device to store at least one electronic personal record having information corresponding to a user; one or more processors coupled with the secure storage device, the one or more processors to maintain permission information for one or more portions of the electronic personal record and to maintain connection information for the one or more portions of the electronic personal record, wherein at least one of the one or more portions of the electronic personal record information from a static document provided by the user and dynamic information obtained via an integration with an external data source, the one or more processors further to evaluate claims on portions of the electronic record from providers utilizing attribute-based security mechanisms, wherein the one or more processors to selectively provide the corresponding portions of the electronic personal record in response to results of the evaluation. 2. The system of claim 1 wherein the providers comprise at least one financial service provider. 3. The system of claim 1 wherein the providers comprise at least one legal service provider. 4. The system of claim 1 wherein the providers comprise at least one medical service provider. 5. The system of claim 1 wherein utilizing the attribute-based security mechanisms comprises accessing corresponding information stored in a blockchain. 6. The system of claim 1 wherein at least one provider is allowed to update the at least one portion of the electronic personal record based on an analysis by the provider of the static document provided by the user and dynamic information obtained via the integration with the external data source. 7. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having stored thereon sequences of instructions that, when executed by one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to: maintain permission information for one or more portions of the electronic personal record; maintain connection information for the one or more portions of the electronic personal record, wherein at least one of the one or more portions of the electronic personal record information from a static document provided by the user and dynamic information obtained via an integration with an external data source, the one or more processors further to evaluate claims on portions of the electronic record from providers utilizing attribute-based security mechanisms; and selectively provide the corresponding portions of the electronic personal record in response to results of the evaluation. 8. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 7 wherein the providers comprise at least one financial service provider. 9. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 7 wherein the providers comprise at least one legal service provider. 10. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 7 wherein the providers comprise at least one medical service provider. 11. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 7 wherein utilizing the attribute-based security mechanisms comprises accessing corresponding information stored in a blockchain. 12. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 7 wherein at least one provider is allowed to update the at least one portion of the electronic personal record based on an analysis by the provider of the static document provided by the user and dynamic information obtained via the integration with the external data source. 13. A computer-implemented method provided by one or more processors, the method comprising: maintaining permission information for one or more portions of the electronic personal record; maintaining connection information for the one or more portions of the electronic personal record, wherein at least one of the one or more portions of the electronic personal record information from a static document provided by the user and dynamic information obtained via an integration with an external data source, the one or more processors further to evaluate claims on portions of the electronic record from providers utilizing attribute-based security mechanisms; and selectively providing the corresponding portions of the electronic personal record in response to results of the evaluation. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 13 wherein the providers comprise at least one financial service provider. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 13 wherein the providers comprise at least one legal service provider. 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 13 wherein the providers comprise at least one medical service provider. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 13 wherein utilizing the attribute-based security mechanisms comprises accessing corresponding information stored in a blockchain. 18. The computer-implemented method of claim 13 wherein at least one provider is allowed to update the at least one portion of the electronic personal record based on an analysis by the provider of the static document provided by the user and dynamic information obtained via the integration with the external data source.
